prompt: Something in shoot_import.py went sideways the moment Saturday's wedding shoot rolled past frame IMG_999. Three symptoms, probably one disease: the contact sheet now shows IMG_1000 BEFORE IMG_998 and IMG_999; the missing-frames report swears half the card is gone when I can see every file sitting right there; and — this is the one that actually cost us — when the retoucher appended an edited export back into the sequence, the tool suggested a filename that was ALREADY ON THE CARD and clobbered a frame. Every card we imported before this weekend was fine, months of them. Those older cards were all DSC_0xxx-style names off the other camera body, if that matters. Repro is python3 test_shoot_import.py. Fix the module so it passes; do not touch the test file.
